steps.title: Connect preference to mating
steps.content: The study found that pied flycatcher nestlings produced more begging calls in response to their own dialect than to nonlocal dialects, showing an early preference for the local dialect. The final sentence adds that song preference plays a role in mate selection.

steps.title: Eliminate unsupported inferences
steps.content: Choice A says the preference likely disappears, which is not supported. Choice B shifts to receiving more food from caretakers. Choice D adds preferences for other local songbirds and nonlocal birds of any species, which the study did not test. The supported inference is that early local-dialect preference may later guide local mate choice. The answer is C.
